data.title: Westpac Announces Cessation of Securities in On-Market Buy-Back
data.description:
The latest announcement is out from Westpac Banking ( (AU:WBC) ).

Westpac Banking Corporation has announced the cessation of 497,280 ordinary fully paid securities as part of an on-market buy-back, effective from April 2, 2025. This move is part of the company’s capital management strategy, potentially impacting its share value and market positioning, and reflects its ongoing efforts to optimize shareholder returns.

More about Westpac Banking

Westpac Banking Corporation is a prominent financial services provider in the banking industry, offering a range of products and services including personal banking, business banking, and wealth management. The company primarily focuses on the Australian market and is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange under the code WBC.

data.title: AsiaPhos Limited Ends Quarterly Financial Reporting
data.description:
AsiaPhos Limited ( (SG:5WV) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AsiaPhos Limited has announced the cessation of its quarterly financial reporting, following an unqualified audit opinion from its current auditor, PKF-CAP LLP, for the financial year 2024. This change aligns with the Catalist Rules, as the company is no longer under the obligation to report quarterly due to the removal of a previous disclaimer of opinion by its former auditor. The company will now report semi-annually, with the next financial statement release scheduled for the period ending 30 June 2025. The Board assures stakeholders of continued transparency and adherence to corporate governance standards.

More about AsiaPhos Limited

AsiaPhos Limited is a company involved in the phosphate industry, focusing on the production and supply of phosphate-based products. The company operates through its subsidiaries and is listed on the Singapore Exchange, targeting markets that require phosphate materials.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: S$5.92M

data.title: Greater Bay Area Dynamic Growth Holding Limited Announces Change in Bermuda Share Registrar
data.description:
The latest announcement is out from Greater Bay Area Dynamic Growth Holding Limited ( (HK:1189) ).

Greater Bay Area Dynamic Growth Holding Limited has announced a change in its Bermuda principal share registrar and transfer agent to Appleby Global Corporate Services (Bermuda) Limited, effective from January 1, 2025. The company’s branch share registrar and transfer office in Hong Kong will continue to be maintained by Tricor Secretaries Limited. This change reflects the company’s ongoing efforts to streamline its operations and could potentially impact its administrative processes and stakeholder interactions.

data.title: Axcap Ventures Restates Financial Statements Following Regulatory Review
data.description:
Netcoins Holdings Inc ( (TSE:AXCP) ) has issued an announcement.

Axcap Ventures Inc., a company listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange, has announced the restatement and refiling of its unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements for the nine months ended September 30, 2024. This action follows a review by the British Columbia Securities Commission and includes an auditor’s review engagement report and management’s discussion and analysis. The restatement reflects changes in the company’s financial position, including adjustments in operating expenses and other income, which have resulted in a reduced net loss for the period. These amendments are part of Axcap’s efforts to ensure compliance and transparency in its financial reporting, potentially impacting its stakeholders by providing a clearer view of its financial health.

Spark’s Take on TSE:AXCP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:AXCP is a Underperform.

Netcoins Holdings Inc. is currently facing substantial financial difficulties, with no revenue generation and persistent losses that severely impact its financial health. While the technical analysis suggests some short-term positive momentum, the company’s negative valuation metrics, including a negative P/E ratio, reflect underlying profitability issues. The strong equity position is a positive aspect but requires a significant operational turnaround to ensure sustainability.

data.title: ASICS Announces Treasury Share Disposal for Employee Incentive Scheme
data.description:
Asics ( (JP:7936) ) has provided an update.

ASICS Corporation has announced the disposal of 191,900 treasury shares as part of a Restricted Share Incentive Scheme for its Employee Shareholding Association. This initiative aims to benefit employees by allowing them to acquire transfer-restricted shares, thereby promoting personal asset building and aligning employee interests with shareholder value. The scheme is designed to enhance corporate value and encourage employee investment in the company’s future performance.

More about Asics

ASICS Corporation operates in the athletic footwear and apparel industry, focusing on designing and manufacturing sports shoes, apparel, and accessories. The company is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and is recognized for its commitment to innovation and quality in sportswear.

data.title: Ridley Corporation Sells Wasleys Feedmill to Baiada for A$22 Million
data.description:
Ridley Corporation Limited ( (AU:RIC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Ridley Corporation Limited has announced the sale of its Wasleys Feedmill in South Australia to the Baiada group for A$22 million, with the transaction expected to complete by June 30, 2025. The sale is part of Ridley’s strategy to strengthen its balance sheet and support growth ambitions, despite the feedmill’s significant contribution to EBITDA. The company anticipates a pre-tax profit from the sale and plans to offset reduced earnings through annual cost savings initiatives, while also navigating challenges in its Ingredient Recovery business due to market restrictions and weather impacts.

More about Ridley Corporation Limited

Ridley Corporation Limited operates in the agriproducts industry, focusing on the production and supply of animal nutrition solutions. The company is known for its feed products and has a significant market presence in Australia.

data.title: Lifezone Metals Reports 2024 Financial Results Amidst Nickel Market Challenges
data.description:
Lifezone Metals Limited ( (LZM) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Lifezone Metals Limited presented to its investors.

Lifezone Metals Limited, a company listed on the NYSE, specializes in cleaner and more responsible metals production and recycling, leveraging its proprietary Hydromet Technology to offer lower energy consumption and emissions compared to traditional smelting processes.

In its 2024 financial results, Lifezone Metals reported a challenging year due to fluctuating nickel prices, largely influenced by increased supply from Indonesia. Despite these challenges, the company remains focused on advancing its Kabanga Nickel Project and a U.S.-based PGM recycling project, while maintaining strict cost controls.

Key financial metrics include a cash balance of $29.3 million as of December 31, 2024, down from $49.4 million the previous year. The company experienced a net cash decrease of $20.1 million, attributed to investments in the Kabanga Nickel Project and a cost reduction program. Financing activities provided a net cash inflow of $48.4 million, primarily from convertible debentures and contributions from Glencore.

Lifezone Metals reported a net loss of $46.3 million for 2024, translating to a basic and diluted loss per share of $0.59. This reflects a significant improvement from the previous year’s loss per share of $5.34, indicating progress in managing operational costs and strategic investments.

Looking ahead, Lifezone Metals remains committed to its long-term growth strategy, focusing on completing the Kabanga Definitive Feasibility Study and further developing its projects to enhance shareholder value and capitalize on the global demand for battery metals.

data.title: Albertsons Reports Steady Growth and Digital Expansion
data.description:
Albertsons Companies, Inc. ( (ACI) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Albertsons Companies, Inc. presented to its investors.

Albertsons Companies, Inc. is a prominent food and drug retailer in the United States, operating over 2,270 stores across 34 states under various well-known banners. The company reported its fourth quarter and full-year fiscal 2024 results, highlighting a steady increase in identical sales and a significant boost in digital sales. Despite a decrease in net income compared to the previous year, Albertsons saw a rise in loyalty members and continued investment in its digital platforms.

In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024, Albertsons achieved a 2.3% increase in identical sales and a 24% rise in digital sales, contributing to net sales of $18.8 billion. The company reported a net income of $172 million, or $0.29 per share, and an adjusted net income of $270 million, or $0.46 per share. For the full year, identical sales grew by 2.0%, while digital sales maintained a 24% increase, resulting in a net income of $959 million.

Key financial metrics included an adjusted EBITDA of $855 million for the fourth quarter and $4,005 million for the fiscal year. The company also announced a 25% increase in its quarterly dividend and a share repurchase program of up to $2 billion. Albertsons continued to invest in its core business, completing 127 store remodels and opening 11 new stores.

Looking ahead, Albertsons is optimistic about fiscal 2025, expecting identical sales growth between 1.5% and 2.5% and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $3.8 billion to $3.9 billion. The company plans to continue its investment in digital platforms and anticipates growth consistent with its long-term goals, despite fiscal 2025 being an investment year.
